2712.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites ten verses in a night shall not be recorded among the heedless. Whoever recites one hundred verses in a night shall be recorded among the devoutly obedient. And whoever recites two hundred verses shall be recorded among the triumphant."
2712.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca sepuluh ayat pada suatu malam, ia tidak akan dicatat sebagai orang yang lalai. Barangsiapa membaca seratus ayat pada suatu malam, ia akan dicatat sebagai orang yang taat. Dan barangsiapa membaca dua ratus ayat, ia akan dicatat sebagai orang yang beruntung."

2713.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites ten verses in a night shall be recorded among those who remember Allah. Whoever recites one hundred verses shall be recorded among the devoutly obedient. And whoever recites from five hundred to a thousand verses shall wake up with a qintar of reward." He was asked: "What is a qintar?" He replied: "A bull's hide filled with gold."
2713.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca sepuluh ayat pada suatu malam, ia akan dicatat sebagai orang yang berzikir. Barangsiapa membaca seratus ayat, ia akan dicatat sebagai orang yang taat. Dan barangsiapa membaca dari lima ratus hingga seribu ayat, ia akan bangun pagi dengan satu qinthar pahala." Ditanya: "Apa itu qinthar?" Ia menjawab: "Kulit lembu yang dipenuhi emas."

2714.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri in a mursal chain that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever recites one hundred verses in a night, the Quran shall not argue against him that night. Whoever recites two hundred verses in a night, the standing of a full night's prayer shall be recorded for him. And whoever recites from five hundred to a thousand verses in a night shall wake up with a qintar in the Hereafter." They asked: "What is a qintar?" He said: "Twelve thousand."
2714.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari al-Hasan al-Basri bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa membaca seratus ayat pada suatu malam, Al-Qur'an tidak akan mendebatnya pada malam itu. Barangsiapa membaca dua ratus ayat pada suatu malam, akan dicatat baginya pahala berdiri semalam penuh. Dan barangsiapa membaca dari lima ratus hingga seribu ayat pada suatu malam, ia akan bangun pagi dengan satu qinthar di akhirat." Mereka bertanya: "Apa itu qinthar?" Beliau menjawab: "Dua belas ribu."

2715.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites three hundred verses in a night, a qintar shall be recorded for him. And whoever recites seven hundred verses" — and Abu Nu'aym was uncertain of what he said about that portion.
2715.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca tiga ratus ayat pada suatu malam, akan dicatat baginya satu qinthar. Dan barangsiapa membaca tujuh ratus ayat" — dan Abu Nu'aim tidak pasti apa yang ia katakan tentang bagian itu.

2716.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites a thousand verses, a qintar of reward shall be recorded for him. And a single qirat from that qintar is worth more than your entire world." He said: "Meaning it exceeds the value of your world."
2716.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca seribu ayat, akan dicatat baginya satu qinthar pahala. Dan satu qirath dari qinthar itu tidak setara dengan dunia kalian." Ia berkata: "Yaitu tidak sebanding dengan dunia kalian."

2717.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tamim al-Dari and Fadala ibn 'Ubayd (may Allah be pleased with them both) that they said: "Whoever recites a thousand verses in a night, a qintar shall be recorded for him. And a single qirat from a qintar is better than this world and all that is in it. And he shall have stored up reward as Allah wills."
2717.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tamim al-Dari dan Fadhalah ibn 'Ubayd (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa keduanya berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca seribu ayat pada suatu malam, akan dicatat baginya satu qinthar. Dan satu qirath dari qinthar itu lebih baik dari dunia dan seisinya. Dan ia akan menyimpan pahala sebagaimana yang Allah kehendaki."

2718.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Whoever recites from a thousand down to five hundred verses, a qintar of reward shall be recorded for him, a single qirat of which is equivalent to a great mound."
2718.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa membaca dari seribu hingga lima ratus ayat, akan dicatat baginya satu qinthar pahala, di mana satu qirath-nya sebanding dengan sebuah bukit yang besar."

2719.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "A qintar is twelve thousand."
2719.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah dua belas ribu."

2720.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Nadra al-'Abdi that he said: "A qintar is a bull's hide filled with gold."
2720.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Nadhrah al-'Abdi bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah kulit lembu yang dipenuhi emas."

2721.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib that he said: "A qintar is forty thousand."
2721.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah empat puluh ribu."

2722.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri that he said: "A qintar is the blood money (diya) of one of you — twelve thousand."
2722.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Hasan al-Basri bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah diyat salah seorang dari kalian — dua belas ribu."

2723.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid that he said: "A qintar is seventy thousand dinars."
2723.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah tujuh puluh ribu dinar."

2724.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "A qintar is twelve hundred uqiyyas."
2724.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah seribu dua ratus uqiyyah."

2725.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid that he said: "A qintar is seventy thousand mithqals."
2725.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid bahwa ia berkata: "Qinthar adalah tujuh puluh ribu mitsqal."

2726.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilaba in a marfu' chain that he said: "Whoever is present when the Quran recitation begins is as though he witnessed a conquest in the path of Allah. And whoever is present at its completion when it is finished is as though he witnessed the spoils being distributed."
2726.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah secara marfu' bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa hadir ketika pembacaan Al-Qur'an dimulai, seolah-olah ia menyaksikan sebuah penaklukan di jalan Allah. Dan barangsiapa hadir saat khatamnya ketika selesai, seolah-olah ia menyaksikan pembagian rampasan perang."

2727.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qatada that he said: "A man used to recite in the mosque of Medina, and Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) had stationed a spy to watch him. When it was the day of his completion, Ibn 'Abbas would rise and go to him."
2727.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qatadah bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang laki-laki dahulu membaca di masjid Madinah, dan Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) telah menempatkan seorang pengamat untuk memantaunya. Apabila tiba hari khatamnya, Ibn 'Abbas bangkit dan pergi kepadanya."

2728.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thabit al-Bunani that he said: "When Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was approaching the completion of the Quran at night, he would leave a portion of it and complete it in the morning, gathering his family together so he could complete it in their presence."
2728.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tsabit al-Bunani bahwa ia berkata: "Apabila Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) hampir menyelesaikan Al-Qur'an di malam hari, ia menyisakan sedikit darinya hingga pagi, lalu mengumpulkan keluarganya agar dapat menyelesaikannya bersama mereka."

2729.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thabit that he said: "Whenever Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) completed the Quran, he would gather his children and the members of his household and make supplication for them."
2729.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tsabit bahwa ia berkata: "Setiap kali Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengkhatam Al-Qur'an, ia mengumpulkan anak-anak dan anggota keluarganya, kemudian berdoa untuk mereka."

2730.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abda that he said: "When a man completes the Quran during the day, the angels invoke blessings upon him until evening. And if he completes it at night, the angels invoke blessings upon him until morning."
2730.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdah bahwa ia berkata: "Apabila seorang laki-laki mengkhatam Al-Qur'an di siang hari, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga sore. Dan jika ia menyelesaikannya di malam hari, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga pagi."

2731.iwh_en: It was narrated from Zurara ibn Awfa in a mursal chain that the Prophet ﷺ was asked: "Which deed is the most excellent?" He said: "Al-hall al-murtahil (the one who camps and then journeys on)." It was asked: "What is al-hall al-murtahil?" He said: "The companion of the Quran who traverses from the beginning of the Quran to its end and from its end back to its beginning — each time he completes it, he sets off again."
2731.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari Zurarah ibn Awfa bahwa Nabi ﷺ ditanya: "Amal apa yang paling utama?" Beliau bersabda: "Al-hall al-murtahil (yang singgah lalu melanjutkan perjalanan)." Ditanya: "Apa itu al-hall al-murtahil?" Beliau bersabda: "Pemilik Al-Qur'an yang menempuh dari awal Al-Qur'an hingga akhirnya dan dari akhirnya kembali ke awalnya — setiap kali ia selesai, ia berangkat lagi."

2732.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibrahim al-Nakha'i that he said: "When a man recites the Quran during the day, the angels invoke blessings upon him until evening. And if he recites it at night, the angels invoke blessings upon him until morning." Sulayman said: "I saw our companions pleased to complete it at the beginning of the day and at the beginning of the night." Another chain narrates the same from Ibrahim without Sulayman's remark.
2732.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibrahim al-Nakha'i bahwa ia berkata: "Apabila seorang laki-laki membaca Al-Qur'an di siang hari, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga sore. Dan jika ia membacanya di malam hari, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga pagi." Sulaiman berkata: "Aku melihat sahabat-sahabat kami senang mengkhatamnya di awal siang dan di awal malam." Sanad lain meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Ibrahim tanpa perkataan Sulaiman.

2733.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muharib ibn Dithar that he said: "Whoever recites the Quran by heart shall have one answered supplication — either in this world or in the Hereafter."
2733.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muharib ibn Dithar bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Al-Qur'an dari hafalannya, ia akan mendapat satu doa yang dikabulkan — entah di dunia ini atau di akhirat."

2734.iwh_en: It was narrated from Talha and 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad that they said: "Whoever recites the Quran by day or night, the angels shall invoke blessings upon him until evening." The other said: "He shall be forgiven."
2734.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Thalhah dan 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad bahwa keduanya berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Al-Qur'an siang atau malam, para malaikat akan bershalawat untuknya hingga sore." Yang lain berkata: "Ia akan diampuni."

2735.iwh_en: It was narrated from Humayd al-A'raj that he said: "Whoever recites the Quran and then makes supplication, four thousand angels say Amin to his supplication."
2735.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Humayd al-A'raj bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Al-Qur'an kemudian berdoa, empat ribu malaikat mengamini doanya."

2736.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid that he said: "Mujahid sent for me saying: 'We only called you because we wanted to complete the Quran, and it has reached us that supplication is answered at the completion of the Quran.' So they made supplications."
2736.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid bahwa ia berkata: "Mujahid mengutus seseorang kepadaku seraya berkata: 'Kami hanya memanggilmu karena kami ingin mengkhatam Al-Qur'an, dan telah sampai kepada kami bahwa doa dikabulkan saat khatam Al-Qur'an.' Maka mereka pun berdoa."

2737.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "If the completion of the Quran falls at the beginning of the night, the angels shall invoke blessings upon him until morning. And if it falls at the end of the night, the angels shall invoke blessings upon him until evening." — He said: "Sometimes one of us would have some remaining, so he would delay it until evening or morning." — Abu Muhammad said: "This narration from Sa'd is excellent."
2737.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Apabila khatam Al-Qur'an jatuh di awal malam, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga pagi. Dan jika jatuh di akhir malam, para malaikat bershalawat untuknya hingga sore." Ia berkata: "Terkadang salah seorang dari kami menyisakan sedikit, lalu ia menundanya hingga sore atau pagi." — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Riwayat ini dari Sa'd adalah baik."

2738.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ata' ibn Yasar that he said: "The bearers of the Quran are the foremost of the people of Paradise."
2738.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Atha' ibn Yasar bahwa ia berkata: "Para penghafal Al-Qur'an adalah pemimpin-pemimpin penduduk surga."

2739.iwh_en: It was narrated that Sa'id ibn Jubayr (may Allah be pleased with him) "used to complete the Quran every two nights."
2739.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Sa'id ibn Jubayr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) "biasa mengkhatam Al-Qur'an setiap dua malam."

2740.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, in how many days should I complete the Quran?' He said: 'Complete it in a month.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I am able to do more.' He said: 'Complete it in twenty-five days.' I said: 'I am able to do more.' He said: 'Complete it in twenty days.' I said: 'I am able to do more.' He said: 'Complete it in fifteen days.' I said: 'I am able to do more.' He said: 'Complete it in ten days.' I said: 'I am able to do more.' He said: 'Complete it in five days.' I said: 'I am able to do more.' He said: 'No.'"
2740.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, dalam berapa hari aku mengkhatam Al-Qur'an?' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam sebulan.' Aku berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku mampu lebih dari itu.' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam dua puluh lima hari.' Aku berkata: 'Aku mampu lebih.' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam dua puluh hari.' Aku berkata: 'Aku mampu lebih.' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam lima belas hari.' Aku berkata: 'Aku mampu lebih.' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam sepuluh hari.' Aku berkata: 'Aku mampu lebih.' Beliau bersabda: 'Khatam dalam lima hari.' Aku berkata: 'Aku mampu lebih.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak.'"

2741.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded me not to complete the Quran in less than three days."
2741.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkanku untuk tidak mengkhatam Al-Qur'an dalam kurang dari tiga hari."

2742.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever does not recite the Quran melodiously is not of us." Ibn 'Uyayna said: "Meaning: to be content with it (to suffice himself with it)." Abu Muhammad said: "People say 'Ubaydullah ibn Abi Nahik."
2742.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Bukan dari kami orang yang tidak melagukan Al-Qur'an." Ibn 'Uyaynah berkata: "Artinya: mencukupkan diri dengannya (merasa cukup dengannya)." Abu Muhammad berkata: "Orang-orang menyebut 'Ubaidullah ibn Abi Nahik."

2743.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tawus in a mursal chain that the Prophet ﷺ was asked: "Which person has the most beautiful voice for the Quran and the most beautiful recitation?" He said: "The one who, when you hear him recite, you feel that he fears Allah." Tawus said: "And Talq was like that."
2743.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari Thawus bahwa Nabi ﷺ ditanya: "Siapakah orang yang paling indah suaranya untuk Al-Qur'an dan paling indah bacaannya?" Beliau bersabda: "Orang yang ketika kamu mendengarnya membaca, kamu merasa bahwa ia takut kepada Allah." Thawus berkata: "Dan Thalq adalah seperti itu."

2744.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has not given permission to anything as He has given permission to a Prophet who recites the Quran melodiously." A companion of his said: "He meant: raises his voice with it."
2744.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah tidak mengizinkan sesuatu sebagaimana Ia mengizinkan seorang nabi yang melagukan Al-Qur'an." Seorang sahabatnya berkata: "Beliau maksudkan: mengeraskan suara dengannya."

2745.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Allah has not given permission to anything as He has given permission to a Prophet who recites the Quran melodiously."
2745.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Allah tidak mengizinkan sesuatu sebagaimana Ia mengizinkan seorang nabi yang melagukan Al-Qur'an."

2746.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salama ibn 'Abd al-Rahman that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say to Abu Musa — who had a beautiful voice for the Quran: "This man has been given one of the reed-flutes of the family of David."
2746.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berkata kepada Abu Musa — yang memiliki suara yang indah untuk Al-Qur'an: "Orang ini telah diberi salah satu seruling dari seruling-seruling keluarga Dawud."

2747.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salama (may Allah be pleased with him) that whenever 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) saw Abu Musa, he would say: "Remind us of our Lord, O Abu Musa." And Abu Musa would recite in his presence.
2747.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) apabila melihat Abu Musa, ia berkata: "Ingatkanlah kami kepada Tuhan kami, wahai Abu Musa." Lalu Abu Musa membaca Al-Qur'an di hadapannya.

2748.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "I would not like to find any of you putting one leg over the other and humming a tune while neglecting to recite Surah al-Baqarah, for the devil flees from the house in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited. The most empty of houses is the house void of the Book of Allah."
2748.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Sungguh aku tidak ingin mendapati salah seorang dari kalian menyilangkan satu kaki di atas yang lain sambil bersenandung dan meninggalkan bacaan Surah al-Baqarah, karena setan lari dari rumah yang dibacakan di dalamnya Surah al-Baqarah. Dan rumah yang paling kosong adalah rumah yang kosong dari Kitabullah."

2749.iwh_en: It was narrated that some of Salim ibn 'Abdullah's family said: "Salama al-Baydaq came to Medina and led them in prayer. It was said to Salim: 'Why don't you come and listen to his recitation?' When Salim reached the door of the mosque and heard his recitation, he turned back and said: 'Singing! Singing!'"
2749.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari sebagian keluarga Salim ibn 'Abdullah bahwa mereka berkata: "Salamah al-Baydaq datang ke Madinah dan memimpin shalat mereka. Dikatakan kepada Salim: 'Mengapa tidak engkau datang dan mendengar bacaannya?' Ketika Salim sampai di pintu masjid dan mendengar bacaannya, ia berbalik dan berkata: 'Nyanyian! Nyanyian!'"

2750.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salama that Abu Musa (may Allah be pleased with him) used to come to 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), and 'Umar would say to him: "Remind us of our Lord." So Abu Musa would recite in his presence.
2750.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Salamah bahwa Abu Musa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) biasa datang kepada 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu), dan 'Umar berkata kepadanya: "Ingatkanlah kami kepada Tuhan kami." Maka Abu Musa pun membaca Al-Qur'an di hadapannya.

2751.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has not given permission to anything as He gives permission to a Prophet who recites the Quran melodiously and raises his voice with it."
2751.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah tidak mengizinkan sesuatu sebagaimana Ia mengizinkan seorang nabi yang melagukan Al-Qur'an dan mengeraskan suaranya dengannya."

2752.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Abu Musa has been given a reed-flute from the reed-flutes of the family of David."
2752.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Abu Musa telah diberi sebuah seruling dari seruling-seruling keluarga Dawud."

2753.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered and heard a man reciting. He said: "Who is this?" It was said: "'Abdullah ibn Qays." He said: "This man has been given a reed-flute from the reed-flutes of the family of David."
2753.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk dan mendengar seorang laki-laki membaca Al-Qur'an. Beliau bersabda: "Siapa ini?" Dikatakan: "'Abdullah ibn Qays." Beliau bersabda: "Orang ini telah diberi sebuah seruling dari seruling-seruling keluarga Dawud."

2754.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Beautify the Quran with your voices."
2754.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Perindahlah Al-Qur'an dengan suara-suara kalian."

2755.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Adorn the Quran with your voices, for a beautiful voice increases the beauty of the Quran.'"
2755.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Hiasilah Al-Qur'an dengan suara-suara kalian, karena suara yang indah menambah keindahan Al-Qur'an.'"

2756.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-A'mash that a man recited before Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) using one of these melodic styles, and Anas disapproved of it. Abu Muhammad said: Others say it was Ghawrak ibn Abi al-Khidrimm.
2756.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-A'mash bahwa seorang laki-laki membaca di hadapan Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menggunakan salah satu gaya melodi seperti ini, dan Anas tidak menyukainya. Abu Muhammad berkata: Yang lain menyebutkan bahwa yang membaca adalah Ghawrak ibn Abi al-Khidrimm.

2757.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Sirin that he said: "They regarded these melodic styles in the Quran as an innovation."
2757.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Sirin bahwa ia berkata: "Mereka memandang gaya-gaya melodi ini dalam membaca Al-Qur'an sebagai suatu hal yang baru (bid'ah)."
